Social media profoundly affects adolescent mental health through complex mechanisms. Platforms facilitate comparison, triggering inadequacy and loneliness. Cyberbullying increases anxiety and depressive symptoms. Online communities offer vital support for marginalized youth. Excessive usage displaces sleep and activity, exacerbating stress. Balancing digital engagement with offline connection is crucial for resilience. Research suggests limiting passive scrolling improves psychological outcomes. Future studies must address long-term impacts to inform policy regarding usage trends. Parents monitor screens actively. Education helps prevent harm too.<|im_end|>
